title: Privileged containers can now restart if the host devices change
body:
If a privileged container is running, stops, and the devices on the host
change, such as a USB device is unplugged, then a container would no
longer start. Previously, the devices from the host were only being
added to the container once: when the container was created. Now, this
happens every time the container starts.

I did this by adding a boolean to the container config that indicates
whether to mount all of the devices or not, which can be set via an option.

During spec generation, if the `MountAllDevices` option is set in the
container config, all host devices are added to the container.

Additionally, a couple of functions from `pkg/specgen/generate/config_linux.go`
were moved into `pkg/util/utils_linux.go` as they were needed in
multiple packages.

Closes #13899

Signed-off-by: Jake Correnti <jcorrenti13@gmail.com>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name.  Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes please follow the kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
Fixed a bug where privileged containers were not able to restart if the host devices changed. (#13899)
```

comments.body:
@edsantiago Any ideas on how we can test this?

@giuseppe PTAL

> @edsantiago Any ideas on how we can test this?
> 
> @giuseppe PTAL

I think this can be tested in e2e by just using `mknod`, running a privileged ctr, starting and stopping it etc. look at https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/0dda4681927b3428a113c81d851d962da8dd7302/test/e2e/pod_create_test.go#L882-L890 @jakecorrenti
It's already gated on `--privileged`, so this should be fine (though maybe we could add a `--device=ALL` in the future to trigger this outside of `--privileged`? Don't see a use for it though...)
I think you can drop the WIP, this looks good on my end (assuming tests pass)

> Can you add a release note for this one (editing the PR description should let you do it)?
> 
> Otherwise LGTM @containers/podman-maintainers PTAL

release note is added
